page_content: CMSC752 Ramsey Theory and its Applications (3 Credits) Theorems about when a coloring of a graph (or other objects) has to yield a nice monochromatic object. Applications will be to computer science theory and to mathematics. Prerequisite: Any CMSC45X course or any 400-level math course. Credit Only Granted for: CMSC858R or CMSC752. Formerly: CMSC858R.
